body.title: Auto-embed on memory creation via Postgres trigger
body.result: Postgres trigger + drain loop; 184k memories backfill-queued; 50 processed/0 errors in test run
body.intention: I intend that any memory row written with non-empty text content is automatically queued for embedding, making semantic search coverage a side-effect of writing rather than a manual step.
body.description:
Add a Postgres trigger on INSERT/UPDATE to clan.memories that pushes a row to an embedding_queue table when content is non-null. The embedding rackmount polls the queue and computes embeddings async. Also backfill existing memories with no embedding. Implementation: DB trigger (not application layer) to avoid touching the high-inertia memory write path in code.

**Affected files:** DB migration (new table: embedding_queue; new trigger on clan.memories); devices/embedding_rackmount/ or equivalent WG device (queue drain loop)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres trigger preferred over application hook to avoid inertia on memory write path
**Scope boundary:** Queue push on creation + drain loop in embedding rackmount + one-time backfill script. Out of scope: code file indexing (separate ticket), routing improvements (separate ticket).
**Completion criteria:** After INSERT into clan.memories with content, embedding_queue contains a pending row within 1s; after rackmount drain, the memory row has a non-null embedding vector; backfill script exits 0 and reports N rows processed.
